Criteria: Hazardous eruption is imminent, underway, or suspected, featuring significant ground/air impacts (e.g., major ashfall, pyroclastic flows, lava flows).
Volcano is exhibiting heightened or escalating unrest with increased potential for eruption, OR a minor eruption is underway posing limited hazards.
Volcano is exhibiting signs of elevated unrest above known background level, or an eruption has decreased significantly but remains monitored.
Volcano is in its typical background, non-eruptive state. If changing from a higher level, activity has returned to background.
Eruption is imminent or underway with significant emission of volcanic ash into the atmosphere, posing a severe threat to aircraft.
Eruption is underway with no or minor ash emission, OR volcano is exhibiting heightened unrest with increased likelihood of eruption.
Volcano is exhibiting signs of elevated unrest above known background levels, or ash emissions have deceased significantly.
Volcano is in a normal, non-eruptive state, or activity has returned to background levels following an eruption.
Significant accumulation of airborne volcanic ash (>= 1/4 inch) is expected or occurring, threatening life, visibility, and infrastructure.
Minor volcanic ash accumulation (< 1/4 inch) or airborne ash suspensions are expected, causing limited visibility and minor respiratory hazards.
Immediate hazard from volcanic mudflows or debris avalanches triggered by rapid snowmelt or heavy rainfall on volcanic deposits.
High-density mixtures of hot lava blocks, pumice, ash, and volcanic gas moving at high speeds down volcanic slopes.
